judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable depth; Report 2 additionally flags a role-name collision issue and missing OIDC permissions block, giving it a slight edge in thoroughness, but both fully satisfy the stated expectations.

report_1.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true" (infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3) — relaxed auth is a staging-only convenience... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" ... must be deleted from prod... SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/" ... Must be removed... LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = "arn:synthetic:secrets:legacy-billing-key" ... should not be recreated in prod

report_2.evidence: AUTH_RELAXED = "true" ... shipping relaxed auth to a real multi-tenant clinic-scheduling production system is a serious security hole. Should be "false". PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" ... must not exist in production. Should be empty/removed. SHADOW_SCORING_SINK ... This needs a prod-specific sink path (or the sink value cleared) — not just the enabled flag. LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ... Production billing should reference its own distinct secret, not reuse... staging's.

report_1.evidence: this URL is baked directly into the static JS bundle — it is not read at runtime. The same built image digest is then reused for both deploy-staging (line 22-28) and deploy-prod (line 29-35)... So the production frontend, once deployed, will ship pointing at https://staging-api.example.com... the frontend must be rebuilt with a prod-specific mode/env file per environment, not promoted as one shared digest

report_2.evidence: Net effect: the production frontend ships with VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com compiled into it — production users' browsers will call the staging API host, because the only build that ever happens is a staging-mode build and it's promoted verbatim to prod.

report_1.evidence: No database migration step anywhere in the pipeline. docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but there is no migration-invoking step in deploy.yml ... should not be trusted without confirming a migration-runner invocation exists.

report_2.evidence: No database migration step, despite docs/prod-runbook.md:6 explicitly claiming "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy." ... the three migrations in backend/migrations/versions/ ... need to be applied to a real prod database before the app can serve traffic, and nothing here does that.

report_1.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 sets the identical string, oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — not environment:production... This line must be changed to repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production before infra/prod is applied.

report_2.evidence: infra/prod/main.tf:3 still reads oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging" — copied from staging and never updated... until infra/prod/main.tf:3 is corrected to environment:production.
